title: Glazed Ethiopian Lamb Stew
description: A savory, glassy-jelly lamb stew with pomegranate molasses, perfect for a warm, romantic dinner.
instructions: Heat the kazan cauldron over medium-high heat and add olive oil.
Add the cubed lamb and sear until browned on all sides; transfer to a plate.
In the same cauldron, sauté the diced onion until translucent.
Stir in minced garlic and grated ginger, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Sprinkle berbere spice blend and black pepper, stirring to coat the aromatics.
Add tomato paste and red wine vinegar, cooking for 2 minutes to meld flavors.
Pour in pomegranate molasses and mix until the sauce thickens slightly.
Return the browned lamb to the cauldron, then pour in chicken broth.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook for 90 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the cover, reduce the liquid until the stew develops a glazed, glassy‑jelly consistency.
Season with salt to taste, stir in fresh parsley, and serve hot.
